tavily.raw: {"query":"Russia largest attack Kyiv 2026 21 killed casualty updates Zelenskyy accelerated weapon systems requested","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://americanpress.com/2026/07/06/russias-missile-drone-attacks-on-ukraine-kill-at-least-21","title":"Russia’s missile, drone attacks on Ukraine kill at least 21 | American Press","content":"## Russia’s missile, drone attacks on Ukraine kill at least 21. Russia unleashed waves of missiles and drones at Ukraine early Monday, killing at least 21 people in attacks that exposed widening gaps in country’s air defenses more than four years into Moscow’s full-scale invasion, authorities said. All of the ballistic missiles launched by Russia struck their targets, underscoring Kyiv’s need for more U.S.-made Patriot interceptor missiles — a point Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy will likely reiterate at a NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, this week. Fifteen people were killed in the capital of Kyiv, which was Russia’s main target, and 56 were injured, according to administrative head Tymur Tkachenko. Another six people were killed in the wider Kyiv region and 21 were inured, according to Mykola Kalashnyk, the head of the regional administration, and other emergency officials. But Russia is now exploiting a different kind of momentum: vulnerabilities in Ukraine’s air defenses, which remain heavily reliant on the Patriot missile systems to intercept ballistic missiles it can rarely shoot down.","score":0.7591723,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.france24.com/en/europe/20260702-night-of-hell-ukraine-russia-vow-escalation-after-massive-attack-kills-21-in-kyiv","title":"'Night of hell': Ukraine, Russia vow escalation after massive attack kills dozens in Kyiv","content":"*   We and our partners use cookies or similar technologies to access and store non-sensitive information such as your IP address. *   By clicking \"Accept\", you consent to the use of cookies or similar technologies by France Médias Monde and its partners. In this photo provided by the Ukrainian Emergency Service, emergency services personnel work to extinguish a fire following a Russian air attack in Kyiv, Ukraine, Wednesday, July 8, 2026. KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian drones and missiles killed four people across Ukraine on Wednesday in attacks before dawn and at midday, as Moscow kept up its pressure on the capital of Kyiv in the latest phase of the over 4-year-old war. Ukraine struck oil refineries in Russia’s Saratov and Tatarstan regions, according to President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, who met with U.S. President Donald Trump on the sidelines of the NATO summit in Turkey. Trump said the U.S. would give a license to Ukraine so it could manufacture Patriot air defense systems to counter Russia. In this photo provided by the Ukrainian Emergency Service, emergency services personnel work to extinguish a fire following a Russian air attack in Chernihiv, Ukraine, Wednesday, July 8, 2026.","score":0.6260562,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.cnn.com/2026/07/03/world/ukraine-russia-kyiv-attack-analysis-intl","title":"Russia’s latest attack on Kyiv was exceptionally deadly – here is why | CNN","content":"Rescuers carry a wounded person from a damaged residential building following missile strikes on the Ukrainian capital of Kyiv on the early hours of Thursday, July 2. Ukraine has endured hundreds of large-scale aerial attacks, but the assault this week was exceptionally lethal, in part because of the targets Moscow chose – residential buildings – and the weapons it used, including ballistic missiles, loitering munitions and jet-powered drones. Analysts from the Institute for the Study of War (ISW), a US-based conflict monitor, said that Russia’s use of jet-powered drones is yet another example of Moscow using tactical and technological innovations in drone systems to “maximize civilian harm during strikes on Ukraine.”. Ihnat said that Russia’s attack on Thursday was also unique because 28 of the 77 missiles it deployed were ballistic missiles, which he said was a “very, very high number.”. The ISW said it is likely that Russia was preparing Thursday’s attack for some time, stockpiling drones and missiles for it in June.","score":0.568632,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.59,"request_id":"f4ebf0e0-289d-4ed0-9d8f-529cab0ebad2"}
